html:
SBS Modified Bitumen for Roofs With Heavy Equipment and Traffic

Modified bitumen still earns its place on a certain kind of Spartanburg County roof: older buildings with dense rooftop equipment, mixed-use properties near Morgan Square with regular maintenance traffic, and low-slope sections where a multi-ply, redundant membrane system holds up better than a single layer. We install and repair SBS modified bitumen using torch, cold-adhesive, or self-adhered application depending on the building and the job conditions.

Why Multi-Ply Redundancy Still Matters

A modified bitumen system typically goes down in a base sheet and a cap sheet, sometimes with an additional ply between, which gives the finished roof more thickness and redundancy than a single-ply membrane at a comparable price point. On roofs with frequent technician traffic to service rooftop units, that redundancy translates into better puncture and wear resistance over time, which matters on the older, equipment-dense buildings scattered around downtown Spartanburg and the surrounding industrial fringe.

Application Method and Job-Site Safety

Torch-applied modified bitumen requires an open flame during installation, which means hot work permits, a documented fire watch during and after application, and coordination with the building's fire protection system where applicable. We follow those protocols on every torch job rather than treating them as optional paperwork, particularly on occupied buildings where a fire watch lapse creates real liability.

Cold-adhesive and self-adhered modified bitumen eliminate the open-flame step entirely, which we recommend on occupied buildings, roofs near sensitive equipment, or jobs where the owner's insurer restricts torch work. The tradeoff is typically a modest increase in material cost and installation time. We walk owners through both options with real cost and schedule numbers so the fire-safety tradeoff is an informed decision rather than a default made for us by whichever crew happens to be available.

Granulated Cap Sheets and Wear Resistance

Most modified bitumen cap sheets carry a mineral granule surface that resists UV degradation and adds a layer of physical protection against foot traffic and debris. Granule color affects reflectivity, and we can quote lighter granule colors where an owner wants to manage rooftop heat gain, though modified bitumen will not match the reflectivity of a white TPO or PVC membrane. We can also pair a modified bitumen cap sheet with a reflective coating in some cases, which narrows the reflectivity gap without switching the owner to a different membrane family entirely.

  • Deck and existing roof evaluation to determine tear-off versus recover
  • Base sheet installation with appropriate lap and fastening method
  • Hot work permit and fire watch documentation for torch-applied jobs
  • Cap sheet application with correct overlap and seal at all laps
  • Flashing at parapets, curbs, and penetrations matched to the field system
  • Post-installation inspection and warranty registration

Where We See This System Most Often

Older commercial buildings near downtown Spartanburg, mixed retail and office properties, and manufacturing buildings with dense rooftop mechanical layouts are where modified bitumen comes up most in our bids. Many of these roofs already carry a modified bitumen system from a previous reroof, and matching or upgrading within the same system family is often the most cost-effective path for the owner. Mixed-use buildings with both office and light retail tenants also tend to have more rooftop equipment per square foot than a single-tenant warehouse, which is another reason the added durability of a multi-ply system pays off on these properties specifically.

Repair Versus Full Recover

Isolated seam failures, blistering, or flashing deterioration on an otherwise sound modified bitumen roof are usually repairable without a full recover. We check the base sheet and insulation condition before recommending anything beyond a targeted repair, since a modified bitumen system in decent shape can often go several more years with the right maintenance rather than requiring a premature tear-off.

Flashing Details on Older Parapet Walls

A large share of the buildings where we install modified bitumen have older masonry or CMU parapet walls with their own maintenance history, and the flashing transition at those walls is frequently where a roof fails first, independent of how the field membrane is holding up. We inspect parapet coping, counterflashing, and wall condition as part of every modified bitumen bid, since a new field membrane tied into a deteriorated parapet detail will leak at that transition regardless of how well the rest of the roof was installed.

Where the parapet itself needs attention beyond the roofing scope, we flag that separately so the owner can budget for masonry or coping repair rather than discovering it mid-project.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is torch-applied modified bitumen safe on an occupied building?

It can be, with proper hot work permitting and a documented fire watch, but we often recommend cold-adhesive or self-adhered application on occupied buildings to remove the open-flame risk entirely.

How does modified bitumen compare to TPO on cost?

Modified bitumen is often competitive with or slightly less expensive than single-ply systems on smaller roofs, though the comparison depends on the number of plies and application method specified.

Can you repair an existing modified bitumen roof instead of replacing it?

In most cases, yes, provided the base sheet and deck are still sound. We assess remaining service life before recommending repair over full replacement.

Does granule color affect how hot the roof gets in summer?

Yes, lighter granule colors reflect more solar heat than dark granules, though the difference is smaller than switching to a reflective single-ply membrane.

What insurance or code requirements apply to torch work?

Requirements vary by jurisdiction and insurer, but hot work permits and fire watch documentation are standard on our torch-applied jobs regardless of specific local requirements.
